Receives the parent group's current children and returns the replacement
array. Runs inside a single tree rewrite, so a batch edit (e.g. grouping or
ungrouping N siblings) costs one O(n) pass instead of N insert/remove
passes. The caller owns IR invariants (unique ids, no cycles) within the
returned array.
Options resolved to concrete values, threaded through emission.
Endpoints of the visible guide line (covers candidate + target rects).
Coordinate of the alignment line in world space.
Rect of the thing being snapped (e.g. a dragging node) in world coords.
When > 0 and no edge snap matches, snap the top-left to this grid size.
Other (unselected) node bounding rects to snap against.
Maximum world-space distance for an edge snap. Default 6.

.

modules; the edge-pair matching is unchanged, only

Apply many commands as one reversible transaction. Wraps them in a

command (so application is all-or-nothing and the inverse is a single
composite) and additionally derives the granular change records. The caller's

is never mutated; a throw from any sub-command propagates without partial
application.

Compute snap deltas + smart-guide overlays for a candidate rect.

Edge-snap-to-other-nodes beats grid snap when both match within threshold;
grid only applies when no edge snap is available on that axis. Guides are
only emitted for edge snaps, never for grid snaps.

Resolve a runtime from zero or more extensions. The zero-extension result is
identical to the static exports; registering custom node kinds rebuilds the
schema pair to include them.

A minimal, framework-free pub/sub for change batches. Works outside React
(e.g. in the host's collab/autosave plugins).
Create a node-kind registry, optionally seeded with built-in definitions.

The top-most node whose box contains

. Nodes are taken in paint order
(later siblings paint on top, matching

), so the last
match wins. Returns null when nothing is hit.

Invert an affine matrix. Throws if the matrix is singular (its determinant is
zero or non-finite), e.g. a zero-scale transform that collapses a dimension.
Allowlist check for an SVG path

string — the one free-text field that
flows into an attribute. Rejects anything outside path commands, numbers,
separators, and scientific-notation exponents.

Nodes selected by a marquee rectangle (in world coordinates). Uses each
node's rotation-aware world AABB;

switches from any-overlap to
fully-enclosed. Returns matches in input order.

Parse + forward-migrate an untrusted/persisted IR to the current schema
version, then validate it. This is the single seam for schema evolution:
when a future

exists, so any other version is
rejected with a clear, actionable error rather than a cryptic schema failure.

Prefer this over a bare

when decoding persisted or
peer-supplied IR (e.g. collaborative editing) so old documents keep loading
as the schema grows.
Multiply two affine matrices. The result applies

to compose a child transform
into its parent's coordinate space.

World-space axis-aligned bounding box of a node, accounting for its
rotation/scale/skew. Pass
